title: contextBlocks whole-block injection is all-or-nothing raw JSON — no field projection, filtering, or pagination
description: wrapBlockContent at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/compile.ts line 162 stringifies the entire block content via `JSON.stringify(content, null, 2)` and wraps it in a context_block XML tag. The bare-string contextBlocks entry form (`contextBlocks: ['requirements']`) injects every field of every item in the block. There is no projection (select specific fields), no filtering (where-clause across items), no pagination (limit/offset), and no per-item macro-rendered option. Template authors can prune in Nunjucks at template-render time, but the full block JSON is read and stringified into the template context regardless of agent needs.

evidence.reference: FB-010 'contextBlocks whole-block injection is all-or-nothing raw JSON'; actual_behavior: contextBlocks: ['requirements'] injects all fields of all items as JSON; even if agent only needs IDs, it receives descriptions, acceptance criteria, status, priority etc for every item

Investigation report FB-010 section confirms wrapBlockContent at compile.ts:162 verbatim; verdict 'Schema + tool surface' — extend bare-string entry to support optional fields[] projection and depth (triggers auto-render); compileAgent auto-applies render_recursive when depth > 0
wrapBlockContent stringifies the entire block via JSON.stringify(content, null, 2) — no projection or filtering applied

impact: Agent dispatches injecting large blocks (40-60 items with rich field schemas) pay substantial token cost on every invocation. The cost is paid even when the agent only needs a thin view (e.g. IDs to avoid duplicates). Template-level Nunjucks pruning trims rendered output but does not avoid reading the full block into the template context.
proposed_resolution: Schema + tool surface per investigation verdict. Schema: extend the contextBlocks entry to accept an object form with optional fields — `{ name: string, fields?: string[], depth?: number }` — with bare-string remaining as sugar for `{ name }`. Tool surface at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/compile.ts line 162: wrapBlockContent reads the optional fields array and projects the block content to those fields before stringify. compileAgent auto-applies render_recursive when depth > 0. Documentation in tool description + schema comment clarifies that contextBlocks accepts bare-string (whole block, no projection) or object form (projected fields, optional auto-render).
